questions.question: Dr. Aris: The experimental results show a clear correlation between sleep deprivation and cognitive decline. Student: _________ Dr. Aris: Exactly. That is why the next phase will focus specifically on long-term memory retention.

options.A: So, you're implying that the results were inconclusive?
options.B: Does this mean the hypothesis has been completely debunked?
options.C: In other words, the less they sleep, the poorer their mental performance?
options.D: Why would the subjects agree to such a rigorous schedule?
options.E: I wonder if the sample size was large enough for such a claim.

explanation.tactic: Diyalog sorularında boşluktan sonraki cümlede yer alan 'Exactly' (Kesinlikle) ifadesi, boşluğa gelecek cümlenin bir önceki cümleyi destekleyen veya özetleyen bir ifade olması gerektiğini gösterir.
explanation.trap: A ve B şıkkı anlamı negatife çevirdiği için 'Exactly' onayı ile çelişmektedir.

questions.question: Sarah: I’m really struggling to synthesize these three different theories for my literature review. James: _________ Sarah: That sounds like a plan. Categorizing them by their methodology might clarify the connections.

options.A: Perhaps you should just focus on the most recent one.
options.B: Have you tried mapping out their core arguments in a Venn diagram?
options.C: Most people find writing literature reviews quite tedious.
options.D: You should probably ask the professor for an extension.
options.E: The library has a few books on general academic writing.

explanation.tactic: Boşluktan sonraki 'That sounds like a plan' ifadesi, James'in bir öneride bulunduğunu netleştirir. B şıkkındaki 'Have you tried...' yapısı bir çözüm önerisidir.
explanation.trap: C şıkkı sadece durum tespiti yapar, bir 'plan' veya çözüm sunmaz.

questions.question: Professor: I noticed you didn't cite the primary source in your latest draft. Student: I thought citing the secondary source that quoted it was sufficient. Professor: _________

options.A: It is, as long as the quote is accurate.
options.B: Primary sources are usually harder to find in the digital archive.
options.C: You always aim to consult the original work to avoid misinterpretation.
options.D: The secondary source is actually more reputable in this specific case.
options.E: I will give you a higher grade if you include more citations.

explanation.tactic: Akademik bağlamda (Professor-Student) profesörün öğrenciye bir gereklilik veya tavsiye bildirmesi beklenir. 'Primary vs Secondary source' tartışmasında her zaman asıl kaynağa gidilmesi akademik bir kuraldır.
explanation.trap: E şıkkı akademik etikten uzak bir rüşvet/pazarlık içerdiği için YDS mantığına aykırıdır.

questions.question: Manager: The board is concerned that our current marketing strategy is too conservative. Consultant: _________ Manager: That’s a valid point. A radical shift might alienate our existing customer base.

options.A: They are right; we need to spend more on digital ads.
options.B: On the contrary, a bold approach is exactly what the market demands.
options.C: However, changing it too abruptly could be perceived as a sign of instability.
options.D: I believe the board hasn't looked at the latest quarterly reports.
options.E: Conservative strategies are always the safest in a volatile market.

explanation.tactic: Boşluktan sonraki 'That's a valid point' (Bu geçerli bir puan/görüş) ve 'radical shift might alienate' (radikal değişim uzaklaştırabilir) ifadeleri, Consultant'ın ani bir değişimin riskinden bahsettiğini gösterir.
explanation.trap: B şıkkı sorudaki anahtar kelime 'conservative' karşıtı olan 'bold' önerir ancak sonraki cümledeki 'alienate' endişesiyle uyuşmaz.

questions.question: Researcher A: The data from the urban study seems to contradict the findings from the rural study. Researcher B: _________ Researcher A: You're right. We shouldn't compare them without accounting for those variables.

options.A: That's because the rural study was conducted ten years ago.
options.B: We should probably just ignore the rural data for now.
options.C: Could that be due to the differing socio-economic backgrounds of the participants?
options.D: Urban planning is much more complex than rural development.
options.E: The software we used for the analysis might have a glitch.

explanation.tactic: Boşluktan sonraki 'accounting for those variables' (şu değişkenleri hesaba katmadan) ifadesi, Researcher B'nin bir değişkenden (variable) bahsettiğini gösterir. C şıkkındaki 'socio-economic backgrounds' bir değişkendir.
explanation.trap: A şıkkı zaman farkından bahseder ancak 'those variables' (çoğul) ifadesini tam karşılamaz.

questions.question: Student A: I find it hard to keep up with the lectures when the professor speaks so fast. Student B: _________ Student A: I’ve tried that, but I still miss the nuances of the discussion.

options.A: Maybe you should record the sessions and listen to them later.
options.B: You should just ask him to slow down during the lecture.
options.C: I think the lecture notes provided online are quite comprehensive.
options.D: Why don't you sit in the front row to hear better?
options.E: The textbook covers exactly what he talks about in class.

questions.question: Librarian: I’m afraid this journal is for reference only and cannot be checked out. Student: _________ Librarian: You can use the scanner in the corner and send the pages to your email.

options.A: Is there any way I can borrow it just for the weekend?
options.B: That's a shame. Is there a digital version available on the website?
options.C: Can I at least take some photos of the relevant articles?
options.D: How am I supposed to finish my research without this book?
options.E: When will the journal be available for general circulation?

questions.question: Interviewer: How do you handle conflict within a team environment? Candidate: _________ Interviewer: That’s a constructive approach. It ensures everyone feels heard.

options.A: I usually report the issue to my supervisor immediately.
options.B: I prefer to work alone to avoid any potential disagreements.
options.C: I try to facilitate an open discussion to identify the root cause of the tension.
options.D: Conflicts are inevitable, so I just wait for them to blow over.
options.E: I always stick to my point of view if I know I am right.

questions.question: Advisor: You’ve met all the requirements for the major, but you still need three credits of a lab science. Student: _________ Advisor: Only if the course content overlaps significantly with our physics department’s syllabus.

options.A: Can I take a biology course instead of chemistry?
options.B: Would the chemistry course I took at my previous university count?
options.C: Is it possible to waive this requirement based on my internship?
options.D: How many hours a week does the physics lab require?
options.E: Are there any online lab options available this semester?

questions.question: Economist A: Implementing a universal basic income could stimulate local economies. Economist B: _________ Economist A: True, but the long-term benefits might outweigh the initial fiscal strain.

options.A: It would certainly reduce the poverty rate significantly.
options.B: Most people would spend the money on essential goods.
options.C: The logistical challenge of distribution is often underestimated.
options.D: However, the tax burden required to fund it could discourage investment.
options.E: I agree that it is the most modern solution to wealth inequality.

questions.question: Student: I'm worried that my thesis topic is too broad for a 10,000-word limit. Professor: _________ Student: That makes sense. I'll narrow my focus to the European market only.

options.A: You should probably increase the word count to 15,000.
options.B: It is better to have too much information than too little.
options.C: Why don't you try to refine your research question to a specific region?
options.D: You can always use more appendices to save space in the main body.
options.E: I think the topic is fine; you just need to write more concisely.

questions.question: Host: Our guest tonight argues that social media is eroding our capacity for deep concentration. Guest: _________ Host: But surely, the ability to multi-task is a necessary skill in the digital age?

options.A: Not exactly; it actually helps us process information faster.
options.B: Indeed, the constant stream of notifications fragments our attention span.
options.C: I believe social media has many benefits for global communication.
options.D: Actually, research shows that students are more focused than ever.
options.E: It’s not just social media; television is also to blame.

questions.question: Colleague A: I think we should postpone the product launch until the software bugs are fixed. Colleague B: _________ Colleague A: I agree. Our reputation is worth more than meeting a temporary deadline.

options.A: But our competitors are releasing a similar product next week.
options.B: The marketing team has already spent the entire budget.
options.C: If we launch now, we can just release a patch later.
options.D: Releasing a faulty product could cause irreversible damage to our brand.
options.E: The developers say they need at least another month.

questions.question: Doctor: Your blood pressure is slightly elevated. Are you under a lot of stress lately? Patient: Yes, I’ve been working overtime to finish a project. Doctor: _________

options.A: You should definitely consider taking a few days off to recuperate.
options.B: I will prescribe some medication to help you sleep better.
options.C: High blood pressure is very common among people your age.
options.D: Do you have any history of heart disease in your family?
options.E: You need to cut down on caffeine and exercise more frequently.

questions.question: Student: I'm not sure if I should take the internship or study abroad next semester. Career Counselor: _________ Student: Actually, the internship is with a multinational firm in London, so I could do both.

options.A: Studying abroad is a once-in-a-lifetime opportunity.
options.B: Which one do you think will look better on your resume?
options.C: Does the internship offer any financial compensation?
options.D: Have you considered whether you can find a middle ground?
options.E: What are the main goals you want to achieve before graduation?

questions.question: Architect: The client wants more natural light in the lobby, but we have structural limitations. Engineer: _________ Architect: That’s an innovative idea. It would bypass the load-bearing walls entirely.

options.A: We could simply install more high-efficiency LED lights.
options.B: What if we incorporated a series of skylights into the roof design?
options.C: I think the client needs to lower their expectations.
options.D: Could we use transparent concrete for the exterior walls?
options.E: We might need to redesign the entire foundation of the building.

questions.question: Peer Reviewer: Your argument is persuasive, but you rely too heavily on anecdotal evidence. Writer: _________ Peer Reviewer: Adding some statistical data from recent surveys would certainly strengthen it.

options.A: I thought personal stories made the paper more engaging.
options.B: How can I make the argument more objective?
options.C: I will remove the anecdotes and replace them with more theory.
options.D: The anecdotes are based on very reliable sources, though.
options.E: Is the tone of the paper too informal for a journal?

questions.question: Travel Agent: This package includes airfare, accommodation, and two guided tours. Customer: _________ Travel Agent: Certainly. We have a 'custom' option where you can select your own activities.

options.A: Is it possible to get a discount if I book for a group?
options.B: Does the price include travel insurance and meals?
options.C: Can I opt out of the tours and reduce the total cost?
options.D: Which hotels are included in the accommodation list?
options.E: I would like to extend the stay by an additional three days.

questions.question: Environmentalist: The increase in carbon emissions is largely due to industrial expansion. Policy Maker: _________ Environmentalist: Precisely. We need to incentivize green energy without hindering growth.

options.A: We should shut down all factories that exceed the limit.
options.B: However, we must balance environmental concerns with economic stability.
options.C: The public doesn't seem to care much about the carbon footprint.
options.D: Are there any alternative energy sources that are actually viable?
options.E: The data suggests that transport is a bigger contributor than industry.

questions.question: Student A: I can't believe I failed the midterm; I studied for three days straight. Student B: _________ Student A: You're right. I spent too much time memorizing dates instead of understanding the causes.

options.A: Maybe you should have studied for a whole week instead.
options.B: The exam was exceptionally difficult for everyone, don't worry.
options.C: It's not about how long you study, but how effectively you use your time.
options.D: I can lend you my notes for the final exam if you want.
options.E: You should ask the professor if you can do an extra credit assignment.

questions.question: Professor: I noticed you missed the seminar on semiotics yesterday. Student: I apologize, Professor. I was dealing with a personal emergency. Professor: _________ Student: That would be very helpful. I will stop by during your consultation hours.

options.A: It is quite alright, but make sure it does not happen again.
options.B: Would you like me to lend you the required reading list?
options.C: I can go over the core concepts with you briefly tomorrow if you'd like.
options.D: The lecture was recorded, so you can watch it online anytime.
options.E: Semiotic theory is foundational for your upcoming term paper.

questions.question: Researcher A: Our data suggests a significant correlation between sleep deprivation and cognitive decline. Researcher B: True, but correlation does not imply causation. Researcher A: _________ Researcher B: Exactly. We need a longitudinal study to confirm that.

options.A: We should disregard the findings until we have more participants.
options.B: Are you suggesting that the methodology was inherently flawed?
options.C: Of course, there might be underlying variables we haven't accounted for.
options.D: I disagree; the statistical significance is too high to be coincidental.
options.E: The participants were all within the same age demographic.

questions.question: Student A: I'm struggling to narrow down my thesis topic for the history department. Student B: Why don't you focus on the socio-economic impact of the Industrial Revolution in Northern England? Student A: _________ Student B: In that case, maybe look into the cultural shifts in the Victorian Era instead.

options.A: That sounds like a fascinating area of research to explore.
options.B: Actually, my supervisor already suggested that specific region.
options.C: I've already written a paper on that for my undergraduate degree.
options.D: I'm more interested in political history than economic trends.
options.E: The library has a vast collection of resources on that topic.

questions.question: Interviewer: How do you handle high-pressure situations in a collaborative environment? Candidate: I tend to prioritize tasks and maintain open communication with my team. Interviewer: _________ Candidate: I try to facilitate a brief meeting to realign our goals and redistribute the workload.

options.A: What do you do if a teammate is not meeting their deadlines?
options.B: How would you react if the project's budget was suddenly cut?
options.C: Could you provide an example of a time you failed to meet a goal?
options.D: What is your preferred method for documenting project progress?
options.E: Do you prefer working alone or as part of a large department?

questions.question: Advisor: I see you've selected several high-level elective courses for next semester. Student: Yes, I want to challenge myself and broaden my academic horizons. Advisor: _________ Student: I see your point. I'll reconsider and perhaps swap one for a foundational course.

options.A: It's important to maintain a high GPA for your scholarship.
options.B: That is a very ambitious goal; just ensure you don't overextend yourself.
options.C: You are required to take at least two language courses this year.
options.D: The registration deadline is tomorrow, so you must decide quickly.
options.E: Most students find those specific courses to be quite manageable.

questions.question: Tutor: Your essay on urban planning is well-researched, but the conclusion is a bit abrupt. Student: I ran out of time and couldn't synthesize the arguments effectively. Tutor: _________ Student: I will definitely do that. It should make the final argument much more persuasive.

options.A: You should have started the research process much earlier.
options.B: Perhaps you can expand on the implications of the zoning laws.
options.C: Try to link your final summary back to the thesis statement provided in the intro.
options.D: The word count is already at the maximum limit allowed.
options.E: Make sure to check your citations for any formatting errors.

questions.question: Colleague A: I think we should adopt a more aggressive marketing strategy for the new software. Colleague B: _________ Colleague A: You're right. A steady, organic growth might be more sustainable in the long run.

options.A: We have plenty of budget left for a massive social media campaign.
options.B: Our competitors are already using very aggressive tactics.
options.C: I believe our current strategy is failing to reach the target audience.
options.D: Don't you think that might alienate our existing conservative client base?
options.E: The software is still in its beta testing phase, so it's too early.

questions.question: Customer: I purchased this laptop last week, but the battery life is significantly shorter than advertised. Shop Assistant: I'm sorry to hear that. Have you been using high-performance settings? Customer: _________ Shop Assistant: Understood. In that case, let's run a diagnostic test to see if the battery is defective.

options.A: No, I've mostly been using it for basic word processing on power-saver mode.
options.B: Yes, I always keep the screen brightness at the maximum level.
options.C: I haven't had the chance to check the settings yet.
options.D: The laptop is quite heavy, which makes it difficult to carry around.
options.E: Is it possible to upgrade the RAM to improve the speed?

questions.question: Librarian: I'm afraid the book you're looking for is currently checked out by another student. Student: That's unfortunate. I really need it for my presentation on Friday. Librarian: _________ Student: That would be perfect. I can use the electronic version in the meantime.

options.A: You can place a hold on it, but it might take two weeks to return.
options.B: We have an e-book version available through the university portal.
options.C: There are other books on the same topic in the 300 section.
options.D: The student who has it usually returns their books late.
options.E: Would you like to speak to the department head about this?

questions.question: Dr. Miller: The results of the blood test indicate a slight iron deficiency. Patient: Is that why I've been feeling so lethargic lately? Dr. Miller: _________ Patient: I'll start making those changes immediately. Should I also take supplements?

options.A: It's a possibility, though we should rule out other factors as well.
options.B: Iron deficiency is very common among people with your diet.
options.C: Yes, it's a primary symptom. I recommend increasing your intake of leafy greens.
options.D: You should get more sleep and try to reduce your stress levels.
options.E: The lab results are not always 100% accurate, you know.

questions.question: Manager: We need to decide on the venue for the annual conference by Friday. Employee: I've narrowed it down to the City Convention Center and the Grand Hotel. Manager: _________ Employee: True, but the Grand Hotel offers a much better catering package.

options.A: The City Convention Center has a much larger capacity for attendees.
options.B: The Grand Hotel is more expensive than we originally budgeted for.
options.C: I think we should look for a venue outside of the city center.
options.D: Both venues are already fully booked for those dates.
options.E: Let's go with the City Convention Center as it's closer to the office.

questions.question: Student A: Do you think the professor will extend the deadline for the group project? Student B: _________ Student A: That's what I thought. We better get to work then.

options.A: He's usually very flexible if you have a valid excuse.
options.B: I heard he's already granted an extension to the other group.
options.C: Highly unlikely, given his strict adherence to the syllabus.
options.D: The deadline is actually next month, so we have plenty of time.
options.E: I'll go and ask him during the break if you want.

questions.question: Tourist: Excuse me, is this the right bus for the National Museum? Local: Yes, but you'll need to get off at the fourth stop. Tourist: _________ Local: No, it's just a five-minute walk from the stop. You can't miss it.

options.A: How much is the fare for a single journey?
options.B: Is it within walking distance from here?
options.C: Is the museum open on Mondays?
options.D: Will I need to take another bus after that?
options.E: Is the stop clearly marked?

questions.question: Mentor: I've noticed you've been hesitant to share your ideas during our brainstorming sessions. Mentee: I guess I'm worried that my suggestions might be too unconventional. Mentor: _________ Mentee: Thank you. I'll try to be more vocal in our next meeting.

options.A: It's better to be safe than sorry when it comes to business.
options.B: In this industry, unconventional thinking is often what leads to innovation.
options.C: You should focus on more practical solutions for the time being.
options.D: I agree, some of your ideas have been a bit off-target.
options.E: The rest of the team is very experienced and might not agree.

questions.question: Landlord: I received a complaint about noise coming from your apartment late last night. Tenant: Oh, I'm sorry. I had a few friends over for a study session. Landlord: _________ Tenant: I understand. I'll make sure it stays quiet after 10 PM from now on.

options.A: Studying is important, but you should do it at the library.
options.B: The neighbors are very sensitive to any kind of noise.
options.C: I'm glad you're taking your studies seriously.
options.D: That's fine, but please keep the volume down during quiet hours.
options.E: I'll have to charge you a fine if it happens again.

questions.question: Peer Reviewer: I think your analysis of the data is solid, but your literature review is missing some key recent studies. Author: _________ Peer Reviewer: I can send you a list of the papers I'm referring to.

options.A: I tried to focus on the most influential works in the field.
options.B: Which specific authors or publications do you think I should include?
options.C: I believe the literature review is comprehensive enough as it is.
options.D: The page limit prevented me from including more sources.
options.E: I'll make sure to update the bibliography before submitting the final version.

questions.question: Travel Agent: The direct flight to Tokyo is quite expensive at this time of year. Customer: Is there a cheaper alternative, even if it takes longer? Travel Agent: _________ Customer: That sounds reasonable. I don't mind a few hours' layover if it saves me money.

options.A: You could fly via Seoul, which would reduce the cost by thirty percent.
options.B: The prices are likely to drop if you wait until next month.
options.C: There are no other flights available for those dates.
options.D: Business class is currently on sale for the same price as economy.
options.E: I recommend booking the direct flight to avoid any delays.

questions.question: Professor: Your presentation was excellent, but you seemed a bit nervous during the Q&A session. Student: I find it difficult to think on my feet when I'm under pressure. Professor: _________ Student: I'll try to do that next time. It should help me feel more prepared.

options.A: You should practice more in front of a mirror.
options.B: It's helpful to anticipate potential questions and prepare answers in advance.
options.C: Don't worry, everyone gets nervous when they're presenting.
options.D: You should have just told the audience you didn't know the answer.
options.E: The Q&A session is the most important part of the presentation.

questions.question: Colleague A: I'm really struggling with this new software update. Everything is in a different place. Colleague B: _________ Colleague A: That would be a lifesaver. When are you free?

options.A: I agree, the new interface is very confusing.
options.B: The IT department is offering a training session next week.
options.C: I've been using it for a few days now; I can show you the basics.
options.D: You should just go back to using the old version.
options.E: It's much faster than the previous version once you get used to it.

questions.question: Interviewer: Where do you see yourself in five years? Candidate: I hope to have taken on more leadership responsibilities within the company. Interviewer: _________ Candidate: I plan to complete a project management certification and seek out mentorship opportunities.

options.A: What specific steps do you plan to take to achieve that goal?
options.B: That's a very ambitious goal for someone with your experience.
options.C: We don't have many leadership positions available at the moment.
options.D: Why do you want to be a leader instead of a specialist?
options.E: How will you handle the increased workload and stress?
